document.description: Telemetry Specification (PMU Events, Metrics and Methodology) for C1-Nano processor

SW_INCR.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, software increment
SW_INCR.description:
Counts software writes to the PMSWINC_EL0 (software PMU increment) register. The PMSWINC_EL0 register is a manually updated counter for use by application software.

This event could be used to measure any user program event, such as accesses to a particular data structure (by writing to the PMSWINC_EL0 register each time the data structure is accessed).

To use the PMSWINC_EL0 register and event, developers must insert instructions that write to the PMSWINC_EL0 register into the source code.

Since the SW_INCR event records writes to the PMSWINC_EL0 register, there is no need to do a read/increment/write sequence to the PMSWINC_EL0 register.

L1I_CACHE_REFILL.title: Level 1 instruction cache refill
L1I_CACHE_REFILL.description: Counts cache line refills in the L1 instruction cache caused by a missed instruction fetch. Instruction fetches may include accessing multiple instructions, but the single cache line allocation is counted once.

L1I_TLB_REFILL.title: Level 1 instruction TLB refill
L1I_TLB_REFILL.description: Counts L1 instruction TLB refills from any Instruction fetch. If there are multiple misses in the TLB that are resolved by the refill, then this event only counts once. This event will not count if the translation table walk results in a fault (such as a translation or access fault), since there is no new translation created for the TLB.

L1D_CACHE_REFILL.title: Level 1 data cache refill
L1D_CACHE_REFILL.description: Counts L1 data cache refills caused by speculatively executed load or store operations that missed in the L1 data cache. This event only counts one event per cache line.

L1D_CACHE.title: Level 1 data cache access
L1D_CACHE.description: Counts L1 data cache accesses from any load/store operations. Atomic operations that resolve in the CPUs caches (near atomic operations) counts as both a write access and read access. Each access to a cache line is counted including the multiple accesses caused by single instructions such as LDM or STM. Each access to other L1 data or unified memory structures, for example refill buffers, write buffers, and write-back buffers, are also counted.

L1D_TLB_REFILL.title: Level 1 data TLB refill
L1D_TLB_REFILL.description: Counts L1 data TLB accesses that resulted in TLB refills. If there are multiple misses in the TLB that are resolved by the refill, then this event only counts once. This event counts for refills caused by preload instructions or hardware prefetch accesses. This event counts regardless of whether the miss hits in L2 or results in a translation table walk. This event will not count if the translation table walk results in a fault (such as a translation or access fault), since there is no new translation created for the TLB. This event will not count on an access from an AT(address translation) instruction.

LD_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, load
LD_RETIRED.description: Counts load instructions that have been architecturally executed.

ST_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, store
ST_RETIRED.description: Counts store operations that have been architecturally executed.

INST_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed
INST_RETIRED.description: Counts instructions that have been architecturally executed.

EXC_TAKEN.title: Exception taken
EXC_TAKEN.description: Counts any taken architecturally visible exceptions such as IRQ, FIQ, SError, and other synchronous exceptions. Exceptions are counted whether or not they are taken locally.

EXC_RETURN.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, exception return
EXC_RETURN.description: Counts any architecturally executed exception return instructions. For example: AArch64: ERET

CID_WRITE_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, write to CONTEXTIDR
CID_WRITE_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed writes to the CONTEXTIDR register, which usually contain the kernel PID and can be output with hardware trace.

PC_WRITE_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, Software change of the PC
PC_WRITE_RETIRED.description: Counts branch instructions that caused a change of Program Counter, which effectively causes a change in the control flow of the program.

BR_RETURN_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, procedure return, taken
BR_RETURN_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed procedure returns, both correctly predicted as well as mispredicted.

BR_MIS_PRED.title: Branch instruction speculatively executed, mispredicted or not predicted
BR_MIS_PRED.description: Counts branches which are speculatively executed and mispredicted.

CPU_CYCLES.title: Cycle
CPU_CYCLES.description: Counts CPU clock cycles (not timer cycles). The clock measured by this event is defined as the physical clock driving the CPU logic.

MEM_ACCESS.title: Data memory access
MEM_ACCESS.description: Counts memory accesses issued by the CPU load store unit, where those accesses are issued due to load or store operations. This event counts memory accesses no matter whether the data is received from any level of cache hierarchy or external memory. If memory accesses are broken up into smaller transactions than what were specified in the load or store instructions, then the event counts those smaller memory transactions.

L1I_CACHE.title: Level 1 instruction cache access
L1I_CACHE.description: Counts instruction fetches which access the L1 instruction cache. Instruction cache accesses caused by cache maintenance operations are not counted.

L1D_CACHE_WB.title: Level 1 data cache write-back
L1D_CACHE_WB.description:
Counts write-backs of data from the L1 data cache to the L2 cache. This occurs when either a dirty cache line is evicted from L1 data cache and allocated in the L2 cache or dirty data is written to the L2 and possibly to the next level of cache. This event counts both victim cache line evictions and cache write-backs from snoops or cache maintenance operations. The following cache operations are not counted:

1. Invalidations which do not result in data being transferred out of the L1 (such as evictions of clean data),
2. Full line writes which write to L2 without writing L1, such as write streaming mode.

L2D_CACHE.title: Level 2 data cache access
L2D_CACHE.description: Counts L2 cache accesses. L2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses. Accesses are for misses in the first level caches or translation resolutions due to accesses. This event also counts write back of dirty data from L1 data cache to the L2 cache.

L2D_CACHE_REFILL.title: Level 2 data cache refill
L2D_CACHE_REFILL.description: Counts L2 cache refills. L2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses. Accesses are for misses in the first level caches or translation resolutions due to accesses. This event also counts write back of dirty data from L1 data cache to the L2 cache.

L2D_CACHE_WB.title: Level 2 data cache write-back
L2D_CACHE_WB.description:
Counts write-backs of data from the L2 cache to outside the CPU as a
result of either:

  - a line being allocated into the L2 cache which then causes a line to
    be evicted
  - cache maintenance operations

BUS_ACCESS.title: Bus access
BUS_ACCESS.description: Counts memory transactions issued by the CPU to the external bus. Each beat of data is counted individually. This event is the sum of BUS_ACCESS_RD and BUS_ACCESS_WR.

MEMORY_ERROR.title: Local memory error
MEMORY_ERROR.description: Counts any detected correctable or uncorrectable physical memory errors (ECC or parity) in protected CPUs RAMs. On the core, this event counts errors in the caches (including data and tag rams). Any detected memory error (from either a speculative and abandoned access, or an architecturally executed access) is counted. Note that errors are only detected when the actual protected memory is accessed by an operation.

INST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed
INST_SPEC.description: Counts operations that have been speculatively executed.

TTBR_WRITE_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, Condition code check pass, write to TTBR
TTBR_WRITE_RETIRED.description: Counts architectural writes to TTBR0/1_EL1. If virtualization host extensions are enabled (by setting the HCR_EL2.E2H bit to 1), then accesses to TTBR0/1_EL1 that are redirected to TTBR0/1_EL2, or accesses to TTBR0/1_EL12, are counted. TTBRn registers are typically updated when the kernel is swapping user-space threads or applications.

BUS_CYCLES.title: Bus cycle
BUS_CYCLES.description: Counts bus cycles in the CPU. Bus cycles represent a clock cycle in which a transaction could be sent or received on the interface from the CPU to the external bus. Since that interface is driven at the same clock speed as the CPU, this event is a duplicate of CPU_CYCLES.

CHAIN.title: Chain a pair of event counters
CHAIN.description: Counts whenever the even numbered PMU counter registers overflow. This event is used when the even/odd pairs of registers are used as a single counter.

L2D_CACHE_ALLOCATE.title: Level 2 data cache allocation without refill
L2D_CACHE_ALLOCATE.description: Counts memory write operations that write a full cache line into the L2 cache without fetching data from outside the L2 cache. These are allocations of cache lines in the L2 cache that are not refills counted by L2D_CACHE_REFILL.

BR_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, branch
BR_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed branches, whether the branch is taken or not. Instructions that explicitly write to the PC are also counted.

BR_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted
BR_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts branches counted by BR_RETIRED which were mispredicted and caused a pipeline flush.

STALL_FRONTEND.title: No operation sent for execution due to the frontend
STALL_FRONTEND.description: Counts cycles when frontend could not send any micro-operations to the dispatch logic because of frontend resource stalls caused by fetch memory latency or branch prediction flow stalls. All the frontend slots were empty during the cycle when this event counts.

STALL_BACKEND.title: No operation sent for execution due to the backend
STALL_BACKEND.description: Counts cycles whenever the dispatch logic is unable to send any micro-operations to the backend of the pipeline because of backend resource constraints. Backend resource constraints can include issue stage fullness, execution stage fullness, or other internal pipeline resource fullness. All the backend slots were empty during the cycle when this event counts.

L1D_TLB.title: Level 1 data TLB access
L1D_TLB.description: Counts L1 data TLB accesses caused by any memory load or store operation. Note that load or store instructions can be broken up into multiple memory operations. This event does not count TLB maintenance operations.

L1I_TLB.title: Level 1 instruction TLB access
L1I_TLB.description: Counts L1 instruction TLB accesses, whether the access hits or misses in the TLB. This event counts both demand accesses and prefetch or preload generated accesses.

L2I_CACHE.title: Level 2 instruction cache access
L2I_CACHE.description: Counts accesses to the L2 cache due to instruction accesses. L2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses. Accesses are for misses in the first level instruction cache.

L2I_CACHE_REFILL.title: Level 2 instruction cache refill
L2I_CACHE_REFILL.description: Counts L2 cache refills due to instruction accesses. L2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses. Accesses are for misses in the L1 instruction cache.

L3D_CACHE_REFILL.title: Level 3 data cache refill
L3D_CACHE_REFILL.description: Counts level 3 accesses that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

L3D_CACHE.title: Level 3 data cache access
L3D_CACHE.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses. level 3 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses. Accesses are for misses in the lower level caches or translation resolutions due to accesses.

L2D_TLB_REFILL.title: Level 2 data TLB refill
L2D_TLB_REFILL.description: Counts L2 TLB refills caused by memory operations from both data and instruction fetch, except for those caused by TLB maintenance operations and hardware prefetches.

L2I_TLB_REFILL.title: Level 2 instruction TLB refill
L2I_TLB_REFILL.description: Counts L2 TLB refills caused by instruction memory accesses except for those caused by TLB maintenance operations.

L2D_TLB.title: Level 2 data TLB access
L2D_TLB.description: Counts L2 TLB accesses except those caused by TLB maintenance operations.

L2I_TLB.title: Level 2 instruction TLB access
L2I_TLB.description: Counts L2 TLB accesses caused by instruction memory access except for those caused by TLB maintenance operations.

REMOTE_ACCESS.title: Access to another socket in a multi-socket system
REMOTE_ACCESS.description: Counts accesses to another chip, which is implemented as a different CMN mesh in the system. If the CHI bus response back to the core indicates that the data source is from another chip (mesh), then the counter is updated. If no data is returned, even if the system snoops another chip/mesh, then the counter is not updated.

LL_CACHE.title: Last level cache access
LL_CACHE.description: Counts transactions that were returned from outside the core cluster. This event counts transactions for external last level cache when the system register IMP_CPUECTLR_EL1.EXTLLC bit is set, otherwise it counts transactions for L3 cache if L2 and L3 caches are present, or for the L2 cache if no L3 is present. Counts the same as LL_CACHE_RD on this CPU.

DTLB_WALK.title: Data TLB access with at least one translation table walk
DTLB_WALK.description: Counts data memory translation table walks caused by a miss in the L2 TLB driven by a memory access. Note that partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

ITLB_WALK.title: Instruction TLB access with at least one translation table walk
ITLB_WALK.description: Counts instruction memory translation table walks caused by a miss in the L2 TLB driven by a memory access. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

LL_CACHE_RD.title: Last level cache access, read
LL_CACHE_RD.description: Counts read transactions that were returned from outside the core cluster. This event counts when the system register IMP_CPUECTLR_EL1.EXTLLC bit is set. This event counts read transactions returned from outside the core if those transactions are either hit in the system level cache or missed in the SLC and are returned from any other external sources.

LL_CACHE_MISS_RD.title: Last level cache miss, read
LL_CACHE_MISS_RD.description: Counts read transactions that were returned from outside the core cluster but missed in the system level cache. This event counts when the system register IMP_CPUECTLR_EL1.EXTLLC bit is set. This event counts read transactions returned from outside the core if those transactions are missed in the System level Cache. The data source of the transaction is indicated by a field in the CHI transaction returning to the CPU. This event does not count reads caused by cache maintenance operations.

REMOTE_ACCESS_RD.title: Access to another socket in a multi-socket system, read
REMOTE_ACCESS_RD.description: Counts read accesses to another chip, which is implemented as a different CMN mesh in the system. If the CHI bus response back to the core indicates that the data source is from another chip (mesh), then the counter is updated. If no data is returned, even if the system snoops another chip/mesh, then the counter is not updated.

L1D_CACHE_LMISS_RD.title: Level 1 data cache long-latency read miss
L1D_CACHE_LMISS_RD.description: Counts cache line refills into the L1 data cache from any memory read operations, that incurred additional latency.

OP_RETIRED.title: Micro-operation architecturally executed
OP_RETIRED.description: Counts micro-operations that are architecturally executed. This is a count of number of micro-operations retired from the commit queue in a single cycle.

OP_SPEC.title: Micro-operation speculatively executed
OP_SPEC.description: Counts micro-operations speculatively executed. This is the count of the number of micro-operations dispatched in a cycle.

STALL.title: No operation sent for execution
STALL.description: Counts cycles when no operations are sent to the dispatch unit from the frontend or from the dispatch unit to the backend for any reason (either frontend or backend stall).

STALL_SLOT_BACKEND.title: No operation sent for execution on a Slot due to the backend
STALL_SLOT_BACKEND.description: Counts slots per cycle in which an operation is available to the dispatch unit, but the operation is not sent from the dispatch unit to the backend due to backend resource constraints.

STALL_SLOT_FRONTEND.title: No operation sent for execution on a Slot due to the frontend
STALL_SLOT_FRONTEND.description: Counts slots per cycle in which no operations are sent to the dispatch logic from the frontend due to frontend resource constraints.

STALL_SLOT.title: No operation sent for execution on a Slot
STALL_SLOT.description: Counts slots per cycle in which no operations are sent to the dispatch logic from the frontend or from the dispatch logic to the backend for any reason (either frontend or backend stall).

L1D_CACHE_RD.title: Level 1 data cache access, read
L1D_CACHE_RD.description: Counts L1 data cache accesses from any load operation. Atomic load operations that resolve in the CPUs caches counts as both a write access and read access.

L1D_CACHE_WR.title: Level 1 data cache access, write
L1D_CACHE_WR.description: Counts L1 data cache accesses generated by store operations. This event also counts accesses caused by a DC ZVA (data cache zero, specified by virtual address) instruction. Near atomic operations that resolve in the CPUs caches count as a write access and read access.

L1D_CACHE_REFILL_RD.title: Level 1 data cache refill, read
L1D_CACHE_REFILL_RD.description: Counts L1 data cache refills caused by speculatively executed load instructions where the memory read operation misses in the L1 data cache. This event only counts one event per cache line.

L1D_CACHE_REFILL_WR.title: Level 1 data cache refill, write
L1D_CACHE_REFILL_WR.description: Counts L1 data cache refills caused by speculatively executed store instructions where the memory write operation misses in the L1 data cache. This event only counts one event per cache line.

L1D_CACHE_REFILL_INNER.title: Level 1 data cache refill, inner
L1D_CACHE_REFILL_INNER.description: Counts L1 data cache refills where the cache line data came from caches inside the immediate cluster of the core.

L1D_CACHE_REFILL_OUTER.title: Level 1 data cache refill, outer
L1D_CACHE_REFILL_OUTER.description: Counts L1 data cache refills for which the cache line data came from outside the immediate cluster of the core, like an SLC in the system interconnect or DRAM.

L1D_CACHE_WB_VICTIM.title: Level 1 data cache write-back, victim
L1D_CACHE_WB_VICTIM.description: Counts cache line evictions from the L1 data cache caused by a new cache line allocation. This event does not count evictions caused by cache maintenance operations.

L2D_CACHE_RD.title: Level 2 data cache access, read
L2D_CACHE_RD.description: Counts L2 cache accesses due to memory read operations. level 2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses, accesses are for misses in the L1 caches or translation resolutions due to accesses.

L2D_CACHE_WR.title: Level 2 data cache access, write
L2D_CACHE_WR.description: Counts L2 cache accesses due to memory write operations. level 2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses, accesses are for misses in the L1 caches or translation resolutions due to accesses.

L2D_CACHE_REFILL_RD.title: Level 2 data cache refill, read
L2D_CACHE_REFILL_RD.description: Counts L2 cache refills due to demand memory read operations. level 2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses, accesses are for misses in the L1 caches or translation resolutions due to accesses.

L2D_CACHE_REFILL_WR.title: Level 2 data cache refill, write
L2D_CACHE_REFILL_WR.description: Counts L2 cache refills due to memory write operations. level 2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses, accesses are for misses in the L1 caches or translation resolutions due to accesses.

L2D_CACHE_WB_VICTIM.title: Level 2 data cache write-back, victim
L2D_CACHE_WB_VICTIM.description: Counts evictions from the L2 cache because of a line being allocated into the L2 cache.

BUS_ACCESS_RD.title: Bus access, read
BUS_ACCESS_RD.description: Counts memory read transactions seen on the external bus. Each beat of data is counted individually.

BUS_ACCESS_WR.title: Bus access, write
BUS_ACCESS_WR.description: Counts memory write transactions seen on the external bus. Each beat of data is counted individually.

MEM_ACCESS_RD.title: Data memory access, read
MEM_ACCESS_RD.description: Counts memory accesses issued by the CPU due to load operations. The event counts any memory load access, no matter whether the data is received from any level of cache hierarchy or external memory. The event also counts atomic load operations. If memory accesses are broken up by the load/store unit into smaller transactions that are issued by the bus interface, then the event counts those smaller transactions.

MEM_ACCESS_WR.title: Data memory access, write
MEM_ACCESS_WR.description: Counts memory accesses issued by the CPU due to store operations. The event counts any memory store access, no matter whether the data is located in any level of cache or external memory. The event also counts atomic load and store operations. If memory accesses are broken up by the load/store unit into smaller transactions that are issued by the bus interface, then the event counts those smaller transactions.

UNALIGNED_LD_SPEC.title: Unaligned access, read
UNALIGNED_LD_SPEC.description: Counts unaligned memory read operations issued by the CPU. This event counts unaligned accesses (as defined by the actual instruction), even if they are subsequently issued as multiple aligned accesses. The event does not count preload operations (PLD, PLI).

UNALIGNED_ST_SPEC.title: Unaligned access, write
UNALIGNED_ST_SPEC.description: Counts unaligned memory write operations issued by the CPU. This event counts unaligned accesses (as defined by the actual instruction), even if they are subsequently issued as multiple aligned accesses.

UNALIGNED_LDST_SPEC.title: Unaligned access
UNALIGNED_LDST_SPEC.description: Counts unaligned memory operations issued by the CPU. This event counts unaligned accesses (as defined by the actual instruction), even if they are subsequently issued as multiple aligned accesses.

LDREX_SPEC.title: Exclusive operation speculatively executed, Load-Exclusive
LDREX_SPEC.description: Counts Load-Exclusive operations that have been speculatively executed.

STREX_PASS_SPEC.title: Exclusive operation speculatively executed, Store-Exclusive pass
STREX_PASS_SPEC.description: Counts store-exclusive operations that have been speculatively executed and have successfully completed the store operation.

STREX_FAIL_SPEC.title: Exclusive operation speculatively executed, Store-Exclusive fail
STREX_FAIL_SPEC.description: Counts store-exclusive operations that have been speculatively executed and have not successfully completed the store operation.

STREX_SPEC.title: Exclusive operation speculatively executed, Store-Exclusive
STREX_SPEC.description: Counts store-exclusive operations that have been speculatively executed.

LD_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, load
LD_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed load operations including Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) load operations.

ST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, store
ST_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed store operations including Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) store operations.

LDST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, load or store
LDST_SPEC.description: Counts load and store operations that have been speculatively executed. Operations that perform a load and a store operation are counted only once

DP_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, integer data processing
DP_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed logical or arithmetic instructions such as MOV/MVN operations.

ASE_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD data processing
ASE_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each operation counted by INST_SPEC that is an Advanced SIMD data-processing
operation. It does not count SVE operations counted by SVE_SPEC, or SME operations counted by
SME_SPEC.

VFP_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, scalar floating-point
VFP_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed floating point operations. This event does not count operations that move data to or from floating point (vector) registers.

PC_WRITE_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Software change of the PC
PC_WRITE_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed operations which cause software changes of the PC. Those operations include all taken branch operations.

CRYPTO_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Cryptographic instruction
CRYPTO_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed cryptographic operations except for PMULL and VMULL operations.

BR_IMMED_SPEC.title: Branch speculatively executed, immediate branch
BR_IMMED_SPEC.description: Counts immediate branch operations which are speculatively executed.

BR_RETURN_SPEC.title: Branch speculatively executed, procedure return
BR_RETURN_SPEC.description: Counts procedure return operations (RET) which are speculatively executed.

BR_INDIRECT_SPEC.title: Branch speculatively executed, indirect branch
BR_INDIRECT_SPEC.description: Counts indirect branch operations including procedure returns, which are speculatively executed. This includes operations that force a software change of the PC, other than exception-generating operations.  For example: BR Xn, RET

ISB_SPEC.title: Barrier speculatively executed, ISB
ISB_SPEC.description: Counts ISB operations that are executed.

DSB_SPEC.title: Barrier speculatively executed, DSB
DSB_SPEC.description: Counts DSB operations that are speculatively issued to Load/Store unit in the CPU.

DMB_SPEC.title: Barrier speculatively executed, DMB
DMB_SPEC.description: Counts DMB operations that are speculatively issued to the Load/Store unit in the CPU. This event does not count implied barriers from load acquire/store release operations.

CSDB_SPEC.title: Barrier speculatively executed, CSDB
CSDB_SPEC.description: Counts CDSB operations that are speculatively issued to the Load/Store unit in the CPU. This event does not count implied barriers from load acquire/store release operations.

EXC_IRQ.title: Exception taken, IRQ
EXC_IRQ.description: Counts IRQ exceptions including the virtual IRQs that are taken locally.

EXC_FIQ.title: Exception taken, FIQ
EXC_FIQ.description: Counts FIQ exceptions including the virtual FIQs that are taken locally.

RC_LD_SPEC.title: Release consistency operation speculatively executed, Load-Acquire
RC_LD_SPEC.description: Counts any load acquire operations that are speculatively executed. For example: LDAR, LDARH, LDARB

RC_ST_SPEC.title: Release consistency operation speculatively executed, Store-Release
RC_ST_SPEC.description: Counts any store release operations that are speculatively executed. For example: STLR, STLRH, STLRB'

L3D_CACHE_RD.title: Level 3 data cache access, read
L3D_CACHE_RD.description: The counter counts each access counted by L3D_CACHE that is a Memory-read operation.

L3D_CACHE_WR.title: Level 3 data cache access, write
L3D_CACHE_WR.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses due to memory write operations.

L3D_CACHE_REFILL_RD.title: Level 3 data cache refill, read
L3D_CACHE_REFILL_RD.description: Counts level 3 accesses that receive data from outside the L3 cache due to a Memory-read operation.

IMP_L2D_WS_MODE.title: L2 cache write streaming mode
IMP_L2D_WS_MODE.description: If the complex is configured with a per-complex L2 cache, this event counts for each cycle where the core is in write streaming mode and is not allocating writes into the L2 cache. If the complex is configured without a per-complex L2 cache, this event counts the cluster cache event, as defined by IMP_L3D_WS_MODE. If neither a per-complex cache or a cluster cache is configured, this event is not implemented.

IMP_L1D_WS_MODE_ENTRY.title: L1 data cache entering write streaming mode
IMP_L1D_WS_MODE_ENTRY.description: This event counts for each entry into write streaming mode.

IMP_L1D_WS_MODE.title: L1 data cache write streaming mode
IMP_L1D_WS_MODE.description: This event counts for each cycle where the core is in write streaming mode and is not allocating writes into the L1 data cache.

IMP_L3D_WS_MODE.title: L3 cache write streaming mode
IMP_L3D_WS_MODE.description: This event counts for each cycle where the core is in write streaming mode and is not allocating writes into the L3 cache. If either the complex is configured without a per-complex L2 cache or the cluster is configured without an L3 cache, this event is not implemented.

IMP_LL_WS_MODE.title: Last level cache write streaming mode
IMP_LL_WS_MODE.description:
If IMP_CPUECTLR_EL1.EXTLLC is set, this event counts for each cycle where the core is in write streaming mode and is not allocating writes into the system cache.
  If IMP_CPUECTLR_EL1.EXTLLC is not set, this event is a duplicate of the L*D_WS_MODE event corresponding to the last level of cache implemented in the cluster. That is:
  - IMP_L3D_WS_MODE, if both per-complex L2 cache and cluster L3 cache are implemented
  - L2D_WS_MODE, if only one of these caches are implemented
  - L1D_WS_MODE if neither is implemented.

IMP_L1D_CACHE_WR_NO_ALLOC.title: Non-allocating L1D_CACHE_WR access
IMP_L1D_CACHE_WR_NO_ALLOC.description: This event counts every memory write operation counted by L1D_CACHE_WR that misses in the cache and does not cause a cache refill.

IMP_L2D_WS_MODE_WR_COUNT.title: L2 cache write streaming mode write
IMP_L2D_WS_MODE_WR_COUNT.description: This event counts each L2 memory write operation affected by, at least, IMP_L2D_WS_MODE.

IMP_L3D_WS_MODE_WR_COUNT.title: L3 cache write streaming mode write
IMP_L3D_WS_MODE_WR_COUNT.description: This event counts each L2 memory write operation affected by, at least, IMP_L3D_WS_MODE.

IMP_LL_WS_MODE_WR_COUNT.title: LL cache write streaming mode write
IMP_LL_WS_MODE_WR_COUNT.description: This event counts each L2 memory write operation affected by IMP_LL_WS_MODE.

IMP_L2D_WALK_TLB.title: L2 TLB walk cache access
IMP_L2D_WALK_TLB.description:
This event counts each access to the TLB walk cache.
This event does not count if the MMU is disabled.

IMP_L2D_WALK_TLB_REFILL.title: L2 TLB walk cache refill
IMP_L2D_WALK_TLB_REFILL.description:
This event counts each refill of the TLB walk cache.
This event does not count if the MMU is disabled.

IMP_L2D_S2_TLB.title: L2 TLB IPA cache access
IMP_L2D_S2_TLB.description:
This event counts on each access to the IPA cache.
If a single translation table walk needs to make multiple accesses to the IPA cache, each access is counted.
If stage 2 translation is disabled, this event does not count.

IMP_L2D_S2_TLB_REFILL.title: L2 TLB IPA cache refill
IMP_L2D_S2_TLB_REFILL.description:
This event counts on each refill of the IPA cache.
If a single translation table walk needs to make multiple accesses to the IPA cache, each access that causes a refill is counted.
If stage 2 translation is disabled, this event does not count.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_STASH_DROPPED.title: L2 cache stash dropped
IMP_L2D_CACHE_STASH_DROPPED.description:
This event counts on each stash request that is received from the interconnect or the Accelerator Coherency Port (ACP), that targets L2 and is dropped due to lack of buffer space to hold the request.
If the core is not configured with a per-complex L2 cache, this event is not implemented.

IMP_L1D_TLB_REFILL_ETS.title: L1D TLB refill due to ETS replay
IMP_L1D_TLB_REFILL_ETS.description: This event counts any refill counted by L1D_TLB_REFILL due to ETS replay.

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_ADDR.title: No operation issued due to the backend, input dependency, address
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_ADDR.description: This event counts every cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK, where there is an input dependency on an address operand. This type of interlock is caused by a load/store instruction waiting for data to calculate the address.

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_VPU_HAZARD.title: No operation issued due to the backend, VPU hazard.
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_VPU_HAZARD.description: This event counts cycles where the core stalls due to contention for the VPU with the other core.

IMP_STALL_SLOT_BACKEND_ILOCK.title: Backend slot stall, input dependency
IMP_STALL_SLOT_BACKEND_ILOCK.description: Counts slots per cycle in which an operation is available to the dispatch unit, and the operation could not be sent to the backend due to an interlock between it and other operations in other slots in the dispatch unit.

IMP_INST_SPEC_LDST_NUKE.title: Instruction re-executed, read-after-read hazard
IMP_INST_SPEC_LDST_NUKE.description: This event counts each instruction which re-executes due to read-after-read hazard

IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_CORRELATING.title: L2 cache refill, hardware correlating prefetcher access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_CORRELATING.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses generated by the hardware correlating prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_SPATIAL.title: L2 cache refill, hardware spatial prefetcher access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_SPATIAL.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses generated by the hardware spatial prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_OFFSET.title: L2 cache refill, hardware offset prefetcher access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_OFFSET.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses generated by the hardware offset prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_HWPRF_STRIDE.title: Level 3 cache access, hardware stride prefetcher
IMP_L3D_CACHE_HWPRF_STRIDE.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses generated by the hardware stride prefetcher, targeting the level 3 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_HWPRF_OFFSET.title: Level 3 cache access, hardware offset prefetcher
IMP_L3D_CACHE_HWPRF_OFFSET.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses generated by the hardware offset prefetcher, targeting the level 3 cache.

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_VPU.title: Backend stall, input dependency from VPU instruction
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_VPU.description: Counts cycles when the backend is stalled because of an instruction interlock, and the source of at least one interlock is a vector or floating point instruction.

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_VPU_ARB.title: Backend stall, busy VPU arbitration
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_VPU_ARB.description: Counts cycles when the backend could not accept any micro-operations because of a lack of arbitration to the shared vector/FP unit (VPU).

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_LS.title: Backend stall, busy LS unit
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_LS.description: Counts cycles when the backend could not accept any micro-operations because of a lack of load-store (LS) execution resources. Refer to STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND to check whether the lack of resources is likely affected by memory resources such as caches or TLB. Refer to UNALIGNED_LDST_SPEC/LDST_SPEC to check whether the lack of resources is linked to load or store alignment.

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_LS.title: Backend stall, input dependency from memory instruction
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_LS.description: Counts cycles when the backend is stalled because of an instruction interlock, and the source of at least one interlock is a memory instruction. Refer to STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND to check whether the interlock is likely affected by memory resources such as caches or TLBs, or is instead primarily affected by load-to-use latency.

IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_LS_INTO_AGU.title: Backend stall, memory instruction input dependency from memory instruction (pointer chase)
IMP_STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_LS_INTO_AGU.description: Counts cycles when the backend is stalled because of an instruction interlock, the source of at least one interlock is a memory instruction, and the destination is the address of another memory instruction. Refer to STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND to check whether the interlock is likely affected by primarily memory resources such as caches or TLBs, or is instead primarily affected by load-to-use latency.

IMP_STALL_SLOT_BACKEND_PORT_CONTENTION.title: Backend slot stall, port contention
IMP_STALL_SLOT_BACKEND_PORT_CONTENTION.description: Counts slots per cycle in which an operation is available to the dispatch unit, and the operation could not be sent to the backend due to port contention between multiple operations in the dispatch unit.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_HWPRF.title: L2 cache hit, hardware prefetch access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_HWPRF.description: Counts L2 cache hits for accesses generated by hardware prefetchers targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_L1DHWPRF.title: L2 cache hit on access, L1D hardware prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_L1DHWPRF.description: Counts L2 cache hits from L1D hardware prefetches.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_L1DHWPRF_FHWPRF.title: L2 cache first hit on access, L1D hardware prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_L1DHWPRF_FHWPRF.description: Counts L2 cache hits due to L1 hardware prefetcher accesses which are the first hit on a cache line prefetched by an L2 hardware prefetcher into the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_PRF.title: L2 cache hit, software or hardware prefetch access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_PRF.description: Counts L2 cache hits counted by either IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_HWPRF or L2D_CACHE_HIT_PRFM.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_CORRELATING.title: L2 cache first hit on prefetched line, hardware correlating prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_CORRELATING.description: Counts L2 cache hits due to demand memory read operations, which are the first demand hit on a cache line prefetched by the hardware correlating prefetcher into the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_OFFSET.title: L2 cache first hit on prefetched line, hardware offset prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_OFFSET.description: Counts L2 cache hits due to demand memory read operations, which are the first demand hit on a cache line prefetched by the hardware offset prefetcher into the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_SPATIAL.title: L2 cache first hit on prefetched line, hardware spatial prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_SPATIAL.description: Counts L2 cache hits due to demand memory read operations, which are the first demand hit on a cache line prefetched by the hardware spatial prefetcher into the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_STRIDE.title: L2 cache first hit on prefetched line, hardware stride prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_STRIDE.description: Counts L2 cache hits due to demand memory read operations, which are the first demand hit on a cache line prefetched by the hardware stride prefetcher into the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_TLB.title: L2 cache first hit on prefetched line, hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HIT_RD_FHWPRF_TLB.description: Counts L2 cache hits due to demand memory read operations, which are the first demand hit on a cache line prefetched by the hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher into the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_CORRELATING.title: L2 cache access, hardware correlating prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_CORRELATING.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by the hardware correlating prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_OFFSET.title: L2 cache access, hardware offset prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_OFFSET.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by the hardware offset prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_SPATIAL.title: L2 cache access, hardware spatial prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_SPATIAL.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by the hardware spatial prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_STRIDE.title: L2 cache access, hardware stride prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_STRIDE.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by the hardware stride prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_TLB.title: L2 cache access, hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_HWPRF_TLB.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by the hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_L1DHWPRF.title: L2 cache access, L1D hardware prefetcher
IMP_L2D_CACHE_L1DHWPRF.description: Counts L2 cache accesses from L1D hardware prefetches.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_STRIDE.title: L2 cache refill, hardware stride prefetcher access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_STRIDE.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses generated by the hardware stride prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_TLB.title: L2 cache refill, hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_HWPRF_TLB.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses generated by the hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher, targeting the L2 cache.

IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_L1DHWPRF.title: L2 cache refill, L1D hardware prefetcher access
IMP_L2D_CACHE_REFILL_L1DHWPRF.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses from L1D hardware prefetches.

IMP_L2D_LFB_HIT_L1DHWPRF_FHWPRF.title: L2 cache line-fill buffer hit on access, L1D hardware prefetcher
IMP_L2D_LFB_HIT_L1DHWPRF_FHWPRF.description: Counts L2 line-fill buffer first hits by L1 hardware prefetcher accesses, where the cache line was fetched by an L2 hardware prefetcher.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_L1DHWPRF.title: Level 3 cache access, L1D hardware prefetcher
IMP_L3D_CACHE_L1DHWPRF.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L1D hardware prefetches.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L1DHWPRF.title: Level 3 cache refill, L1D hardware prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L1DHWPRF.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L1D hardware prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF.title: Level 3 cache refill, L2D hardware prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L2D hardware prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache. Refer to child events for a breakdown.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_CORRELATIN.title: Level 3 cache refill, L2D hardware correlating prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_CORRELATIN.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L2D hardware correlating prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_OFFSET.title: Level 3 cache refill, L2D hardware offset prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_OFFSET.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L2D hardware offset prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_SPATIAL.title: Level 3 cache refill, L2D hardware spatial prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_SPATIAL.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L2D hardware spatial prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_STRIDE.title: Level 3 cache refill, L2D hardware stride prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_STRIDE.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L2D hardware stride prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_TLB.title: Level 3 cache refill, L2D hardware TLB descriptor prefetcher access
IMP_L3D_CACHE_REFILL_L2DHWPRF_TLB.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses from L2D hardware TLB descriptor prefetches that receive data from outside the L3 cache.

STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit busy
STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME.description:
The counter counts each PE cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_CPUBOUND when the PEs backend was stalled
due SME instructions not able to progress, typically:

- When waiting for SME2 unit arbitration
- Because of SME2 unit backpressure
- Because instructions cannot be sent to the SME2 unit due to dependencies, commit, etc.

STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CMEBOUND.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit backpressure
STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CMEBOUND.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME when the SME2 unit
causes backpressure and does not accept instructions.

Notes:

- This event counts independently of oldest instruction being ready to be sent
  to the SME2 unit
- This event does not count when the CPU is waiting for arbitration, and
  STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_ARB is counting.

STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_ARB.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit arbitration
STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_ARB.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME when oldest SME
instruction cannot be sent because it is waiting for arbitration.

STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_CPUBOUND.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit stalled by CPU
STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_CPUBOUND.description:
The counter counts each PE cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME when not
counted by STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_BOUND or STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_ARB.

This can typically be due to:

- Instruction waiting for commit point being reached
- A register dependency prevents the SSVE oldest instruction to be sent
- A control packet needs to be sent

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME.title: Backend stall, input dependency from SME2 unit
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK when a CPU instruction
waits for a register update from the SME2 unit, including predicate, flags and GPR
updates.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME_GPR.title: Backend stall, input dependency from SME2 unit general purpose register
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME_GPR.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME when dependency is on
a GPR register.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME_PRED.title: Backend stall, input dependency from SME2 unit predicate register
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME_PRED.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME when dependency is on
a Predicate register.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME_FLAGS.title: Backend stall, input dependency from SME2 unit flag register
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME_FLAGS.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_FROM_CME when dependency is on
flags register.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME.title: Backend stall, SME2 unit input dependency from CPU
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK when oldest SME instruction
is busy due to a data hazard between the SME2 unit and the CPU, including predicate updates and GPR
updates.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME_GPR.title: Backend stall, SME2 unit input dependency from CPU on general purpose register
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME_GPR.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME when oldest
SME instruction is busy due to a GPR dependency.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME_PRED.title: Backend stall, SME2 unit input dependency from CPU predicate register
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME_PRED.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME when operations
when oldest SME instruction is busy due to a predicate register dependency.

STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME_FLAGS.title: Backend stall, SME2 unit input dependency from CPU on flags
STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME_FLAGS.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_ILOCK_TO_CME when operations
when oldest SME instruction is busy due to flags dependency.

STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME_LSRT_FULL.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit stalled on LSRT full
STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME_LSRT_FULL.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND when
at least one Streaming SVE instruction is waiting for an entry being freed to be
allocated into the LSRT.

STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME_BARRIER.title: Backend stall cycles, barrier stalled by SME2 unit
STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME_BARRIER.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND when a barrier instruction
is executed and waits for completions from SME load/store transactions.

This includes effect due to store-release or load-acquire semantic.

STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME_HZ_ON_CPU.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit stalled by CPU memory hazard
STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME_HZ_ON_CPU.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND when
at least one Streaming SVE load/store instruction is waiting for resolution from an address
hazard. This could be used to detect cases for which the CPU and SME unit are making
overlapping accesses, that is, both are accessing the same location, and the SME unit
cannot accept the operation to preserve the ordering of memory effects for the location
required by the architecture.

STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CPU_HZ_ON_CME.title: Backend stall cycles, CPU stalled by SME2 unit memory hazard
STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CPU_HZ_ON_CME.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND when
at least one CPU load/store instruction is waiting for resolution from an address
hazard. This could be used to detect cases for which the CPU and SME unit are making
overlapping accesses, that is, both are accessing the same location, and the CPU
cannot execute the operation to preserve the ordering of memory effects for the location
required by the architecture.

STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME.title: Backend stall cycles, SME2 unit stalled on memory hazard or LSRT full
STALL_BACKEND_MEM_CME.description:
The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_MEMBOUND when
at least one Streaming SVE instruction is waiting for an entry being freed to be
allocated into the LSRT or an address hazard to be resolved.

STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_GPR.title: Backend stall cycles, CPU waiting for SME2 unit general purpose register response
STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_GPR.description: The counter counts each CPU cycle counted by STALL_BACKEND_BUSY_CME_CPUBOUND while waiting for a GPR response from SME2 unit

SM_ACTIVE_CYCLES.title: Cycles PSTATE.SM enabled
SM_ACTIVE_CYCLES.description: The counter counts each cycle counted by `CPU_CYCLES` when `PSTATE.SM` was enabled.

CYCLES_CME_ALLOC.title: Cycles SME2 unit allocated
CYCLES_CME_ALLOC.description:
The counter counts each PE cycle counted by CPU_CYCLES where the CPU had an granted
arbitration to the SME2 unit, such that the SME2 and Streaming SVE state of the CPU is held in that SME2 unit.
It does not count cycles during which a CPU has requested arbitration and is waiting for
acknowledgement.

CYCLES_ARB_PENDING_CME.title: Cycles SME2 unit arbitration pending
CYCLES_ARB_PENDING_CME.description:
The counter counts each PE cycle counted by CPU_CYCLES where the CPU is in waiting for arbitration
while attempting to access the SME2 unit. If can be due an initial arbitration request or contention.

CYCLES_CME_RECONNECT_PENDING.title: Cycles SME2 unit reconnect pending
CYCLES_CME_RECONNECT_PENDING.description:
The counter counts each PE cycle counted by CYCLES_ARB_PENDING_CME where the CPU is in waiting for
arbitration due to contention, when it was previously arbitrated to a SME2 unit but arbitration was
granted to another CPU.

ARB_CME_COUNT.title: SME2 unit arbitration requests
ARB_CME_COUNT.description:
The counter counts the number of times an arbitration to SME2 unit is requested, either an initial
request, or a reconnect request due to contention.

RECONNECT_CME_COUNT.title: SME2 unit reconnect requests
RECONNECT_CME_COUNT.description:
The counter counts the number of times the CPU needs to request arbitration following a
disconnect due to contention.

OP_CME_ISSUE.title: SME operation issued
OP_CME_ISSUE.description:
The counter counts each operation counted by OP_ISSUE that was issued to a streaming mode
compute unit.

The definition of which operations are issued to an SME2 unit is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED.
The maximum value by which the counter could increment by in a single cycle is IMPLEMENTATION
DEFINED.

SSVE_INST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Streaming SVE, including load and store
SSVE_INST_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each instruction counted by SVE_INST_SPEC when the CPU executes in
Streaming mode.

SSVE_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Streaming SVE
SSVE_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each operation counted by SVE_SPEC specifically in Streaming
mode.

SSVE_PRED_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Streaming SVE predicated
SSVE_PRED_SPEC.description:
Counts operations counted by SVE_PRED_SPEC, but in Streaming mode only.

Note: this counts SME operations requiring PSTATE.ZA to be set, including 2D operations.

SSVE_PRED_EMPTY_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Streaming SVE predicated with no active predicates
SSVE_PRED_EMPTY_SPEC.description: Counts operations counted by SVE_PRED_EMPTY_SPEC, but in Streaming mode only.

SSVE_PRED_FULL_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Streaming SVE predicated with all active predicates
SSVE_PRED_FULL_SPEC.description:
Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with all
predicate elements active, specifically in Streaming mode.

SSVE_PRED_NOT_FULL_SPEC.title: Streaming SVE predicated operations Speculatively executed with no active or partially active predicates
SSVE_PRED_NOT_FULL_SPEC.description:
Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with at least
one non active predicate elements, specifically in Streaming mode.

SSVE_PRED_PARTIAL_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Streaming SVE predicated with partially active predicates
SSVE_PRED_PARTIAL_SPEC.description:
Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with at least
one but not all active predicate elements, specifically in streaming
mode.

SAMPLE_POP.title: Statistical Profiling sample population
SAMPLE_POP.description: Counts statistical profiling sample population, the count of all operations that could be sampled but may or may not be chosen for sampling.

SAMPLE_FEED.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken
SAMPLE_FEED.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken for sampling.

SAMPLE_FILTRATE.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken and not removed by filtering
SAMPLE_FILTRATE.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are not removed by filtering.

SAMPLE_COLLISION.title: Statistical Profiling sample collided with previous sample
SAMPLE_COLLISION.description: Counts statistical profiling samples that have collided with a previous sample and so therefore not taken.

STALL_BACKEND_MEM.title: Memory stall cycles
STALL_BACKEND_MEM.description: Counts cycles when the backend is stalled, and there is a demand data miss in the last level core cache.

L1I_CACHE_LMISS.title: Level 1 instruction cache long-latency miss
L1I_CACHE_LMISS.description: Counts cache line refills into the L1 instruction cache, that incurred additional latency.

L2D_CACHE_LMISS_RD.title: Level 2 data cache long-latency read miss
L2D_CACHE_LMISS_RD.description: Counts cache line refills into the L2 unified cache from any memory read operations that incurred additional latency.

L3D_CACHE_LMISS_RD.title: Level 3 data cache long-latency read miss
L3D_CACHE_LMISS_RD.description: Counts any cache line refill into the level 3 cache from memory read operations that incurred additional latency.

TRB_WRAP.title: Trace buffer current write pointer wrapped
TRB_WRAP.description: This event is generated each time the current write pointer is wrapped to the base pointer.

PMU_OVFS.title: PMU overflow, counters accessible to EL1 and EL0
PMU_OVFS.description: This event is generated each time an event causes a PMEVCTNR<n>_EL1 counter overflow when PMINTENSET_EL1[n] is set to 1, for each implemented PMU counter n in the range 0 <= n < UInt(MDCR_EL2.HPMN), and the Cycle Counter (n = 31).

TRB_TRIG.title: Trace buffer Trigger Event
TRB_TRIG.description: This event is generated when a Trace Buffer Extension Trigger Event occurs.

PMU_HOVFS.title: PMU overflow, counters reserved for use by EL2
PMU_HOVFS.description: This event is generated each time an event causes a PMEVCTNR<n>_EL1 counter overflow when PMINTENSET_EL1[n] is set to 1, for each implemented PMU counter n in the range UInt(MDCR_EL2.HPMN) <= n < UInt(PMCR_EL0.N). This event is not transmitted to a PE Trace Unit while TRCFR_EL2.E2TRE == 0b0.

TRCEXTOUT0.title: Trace unit external output 0
TRCEXTOUT0.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled by ETE external event 0.

TRCEXTOUT1.title: Trace unit external output 1
TRCEXTOUT1.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled by ETE external event 1.

TRCEXTOUT2.title: Trace unit external output 2
TRCEXTOUT2.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled by ETE external event 2.

TRCEXTOUT3.title: Trace unit external output 3
TRCEXTOUT3.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled by ETE external event 3.

CTI_TRIGOUT4.title: Cross-trigger Interface output trigger 4
CTI_TRIGOUT4.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled on CTI output trigger 4.

CTI_TRIGOUT5.title: Cross-trigger Interface output trigger 5
CTI_TRIGOUT5.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled on CTI output trigger 5.

CTI_TRIGOUT6.title: Cross-trigger Interface output trigger 6
CTI_TRIGOUT6.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled on CTI output trigger 6.

CTI_TRIGOUT7.title: Cross-trigger Interface output trigger 7
CTI_TRIGOUT7.description: This event is generated each time an event is signaled on CTI output trigger 7.

LDST_ALIGN_LAT.title: Access with additional latency from alignment
LDST_ALIGN_LAT.description: Counts the number of memory read and write accesses in a cycle that incurred additional latency, due to the alignment of the address and the size of data being accessed, which results in store crossing a single cache line.

LD_ALIGN_LAT.title: Load with additional latency from alignment
LD_ALIGN_LAT.description: Counts the number of memory read accesses in a cycle that incurred additional latency, due to the alignment of the address and size of data being accessed, which results in load crossing a single cache line.

ST_ALIGN_LAT.title: Store with additional latency from alignment
ST_ALIGN_LAT.description: Counts the number of memory write access in a cycle that incurred additional latency, due to the alignment of the address and size of data being accessed incurred additional latency.

MEM_ACCESS_CHECKED.title: Checked data memory access
MEM_ACCESS_CHECKED.description: Counts the number of memory read and write accesses in a cycle that that are tag checked by the Memory Tagging Extension (MTE).

MEM_ACCESS_CHECKED_RD.title: Checked data memory access, read
MEM_ACCESS_CHECKED_RD.description: Counts the number of memory read accesses in a cycle that are tag checked by the Memory Tagging Extension (MTE).

MEM_ACCESS_CHECKED_WR.title: Checked data memory access, write
MEM_ACCESS_CHECKED_WR.description: Counts the number of memory write accesses in a cycle that is tag checked by the Memory Tagging Extension (MTE).

SIMD_INST_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, SIMD
SIMD_INST_RETIRED.description: Counts executed operations that are SIMD or SVE vector operations or Advanced SIMD non-scalar operations.

SIMD_INST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SIMD
SIMD_INST_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed operations that are SIMD or SVE vector operations or Advanced SIMD non-scalar operations.

ASE_INST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD
ASE_INST_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed operation due to an A64 Advanced SIMD
instruction.

It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the counter counts operations due to Advanced SIMD
scalar instructions.

SVE_INST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE, including load and store
SVE_INST_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed operation due to an SVE instruction.

It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the counter counts operations due to non-SIMD SVE
instructions.

Instructions classified as SME instructions and counted by SME_INST_SPEC are not counted
by this event.

FP_HP_SPEC.title: Floating-point operation speculatively executed, half precision
FP_HP_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed half precision floating point operations.

FP_SP_SPEC.title: Floating-point operation speculatively executed, single precision
FP_SP_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed single precision floating point operations.

FP_DP_SPEC.title: Floating-point operation speculatively executed, double precision
FP_DP_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed double precision floating point operations.

SVE_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE
SVE_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each operation counted by INST_SPEC that is a scalable vector
data processing operation.

It does not count:

- SME operations counted by SME_SPEC.
- Neon operation counted by ASE_SPEC
- Load/store operations

ASE_SVE_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD or SVE data processing
ASE_SVE_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each operation counted by INST_SPEC that is an Advanced SIMD or scalable vector
data processing operation.

It does not count:

- SME operations counted by SME_SPEC.
- Load/store operations

See ASE_SPEC and SVE_SPEC for these classifications.

SVE_PRED_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE predicated
SVE_PRED_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations.

SVE_PRED_EMPTY_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE predicated with no active predicates
SVE_PRED_EMPTY_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with no active predicate elements.

SVE_PRED_FULL_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE predicated with all active predicates
SVE_PRED_FULL_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with all predicate elements active.

SVE_PRED_PARTIAL_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE predicated with partially active predicates
SVE_PRED_PARTIAL_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with at least one but not all active predicate elements.

SVE_UNPRED_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE unpredicated
SVE_UNPRED_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed SIMD data-processing, load, or store operation due
to an SVE instruction without a Governing predicate operand.

This counts the SME operations requiring PSTATE.ZA to be set.  It does not count Advanced SIMD operations.

SVE_PRED_NOT_FULL_SPEC.title: SVE predicated operations speculatively executed with no active or partially active predicates
SVE_PRED_NOT_FULL_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed predicated SVE operations with at least one non active predicate elements.

SVE_LDST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE load, store, or prefetch
SVE_LDST_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed load, store, or prefetch operation counted by any
of SVE_LD_SPEC, SVE_PRF_SPEC, or SVE_ST_SPEC.

This includes Load/Store operations to Z register but does not count Load/Store operations to
ZT and ZA registers.

SVE_LD_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE load
SVE_LD_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed operation that reads from memory due to an SVE
load instruction.

SVE_ST_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE store
SVE_ST_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed operation that writes to memory due to an SVE
store instruction.

SVE_PRF_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE prefetch
SVE_PRF_SPEC.description:
The counter counts each Speculatively executed prefetch operation due to any of the following A64
instructions:

- SVE: PRFB, PRFD, PRFH, or PRFW.

SVE_LDFF_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE first-fault load
SVE_LDFF_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed SVE first fault or non-fault load operations.

SVE_LDFF_FAULT_SPEC.title: Operation speculatively executed, SVE first-fault load which set FFR bit to 0b0
SVE_LDFF_FAULT_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed SVE first fault or non-fault load operations that clear at least one bit in the FFR.

ASE_SVE_INT8_SPEC.title: Integer operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD or SVE 8-bit
ASE_SVE_INT8_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed Advanced SIMD or SVE integer operations with the largest data type an 8-bit integer.

ASE_SVE_INT16_SPEC.title: Integer operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD or SVE 16-bit
ASE_SVE_INT16_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed Advanced SIMD or SVE integer operations with the largest data type a 16-bit integer.

ASE_SVE_INT32_SPEC.title: Integer operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD or SVE 32-bit
ASE_SVE_INT32_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed Advanced SIMD or SVE integer operations with the largest data type a 32-bit integer.

ASE_SVE_INT64_SPEC.title: Integer operation speculatively executed, Advanced SIMD or SVE 64-bit
ASE_SVE_INT64_SPEC.description: Counts speculatively executed Advanced SIMD or SVE integer operations with the largest data type a 64-bit integer.

BR_SKIP_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, not taken
BR_SKIP_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed branches that were not taken.

BR_IMMED_TAKEN_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, immediate, taken
BR_IMMED_TAKEN_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed immediate branches that were conditional and taken.

BR_INDNR_TAKEN_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, indirect excluding procedure return, taken
BR_INDNR_TAKEN_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed indirect branches excluding procedure returns that were taken.

BR_IMMED_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted immediate
BR_IMMED_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed direct branches that were correctly predicted.

BR_IMMED_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted immediate
BR_IMMED_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed direct branches that were mispredicted and caused a pipeline flush.

BR_IND_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted indirect
BR_IND_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed indirect branches including procedure returns that were correctly predicted.

BR_IND_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted indirect
BR_IND_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed indirect branches including procedure returns that were mispredicted and caused a pipeline flush.

BR_RETURN_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted procedure return
BR_RETURN_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed procedure returns that were correctly predicted.

BR_RETURN_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted procedure return
BR_RETURN_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed procedure returns that were mispredicted and caused a pipeline flush.

BR_INDNR_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted indirect excluding procedure return
BR_INDNR_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed indirect branches excluding procedure returns that were correctly predicted.

BR_INDNR_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted indirect excluding procedure return
BR_INDNR_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed indirect branches excluding procedure returns that were mispredicted and caused a pipeline flush.

BR_TAKEN_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted branch, taken
BR_TAKEN_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed branches that were taken and were correctly predicted.

BR_TAKEN_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted branch, taken
BR_TAKEN_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed branches that were taken and were mispredicted causing a pipeline flush.

BR_SKIP_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted branch, not taken
BR_SKIP_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed branches that were not taken and were correctly predicted.

BR_SKIP_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, mispredicted branch, not taken
BR_SKIP_MIS_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed branches that were not taken and were mispredicted causing a pipeline flush.

BR_PRED_RETIRED.title: Branch instruction architecturally executed, predicted branch
BR_PRED_RETIRED.description: Counts branch instructions counted by BR_RETIRED which were correctly predicted.

BR_IND_RETIRED.title: Instruction architecturally executed, indirect branch
BR_IND_RETIRED.description: Counts architecturally executed indirect branches including procedure returns.

INST_FETCH_PERCYC.title: Event in progress, INST FETCH
INST_FETCH_PERCYC.description: Counts number of instruction fetches outstanding per cycle, which will provide an average latency of instruction fetch.

MEM_ACCESS_RD_PERCYC.title: Event in progress, MEM ACCESS RD
MEM_ACCESS_RD_PERCYC.description: Counts the number of outstanding loads or memory read accesses per cycle.

INST_FETCH.title: Instruction memory access
INST_FETCH.description: Counts instruction memory accesses in a given cycle.

BUS_REQ_RD_PERCYC.title: Bus read transactions in progress
BUS_REQ_RD_PERCYC.description: The counter counts by the number of bus read transactions counted by BUS_REQ_RD in progress on each cycle.

DTLB_WALK_PERCYC.title: Event in progress, DTLB WALK
DTLB_WALK_PERCYC.description: Counts number of DTLB translation table walks in progress in a cycle.

ITLB_WALK_PERCYC.title: Event in progress, ITLB WALK
ITLB_WALK_PERCYC.description: Counts number of ITLB translation table walks in progress in a cycle.

SAMPLE_FEED_BR.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken, branch
SAMPLE_FEED_BR.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are branches.

SAMPLE_FEED_LD.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken, load
SAMPLE_FEED_LD.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are loads or load atomic operations.

SAMPLE_FEED_ST.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken, store
SAMPLE_FEED_ST.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are stores or store atomic operations.

SAMPLE_FEED_OP.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken, matching operation type
SAMPLE_FEED_OP.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are matching any operation type filters supported.

SAMPLE_FEED_EVENT.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken, matching events
SAMPLE_FEED_EVENT.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are matching event packet filter constraints.

SAMPLE_FEED_LAT.title: Statistical Profiling sample taken, exceeding minimum latency
SAMPLE_FEED_LAT.description: Counts statistical profiling samples taken which are exceeding minimum latency set by operation latency filter constraints.

L1I_TLB_RD.title: Level 1 instruction TLB demand access
L1I_TLB_RD.description: Counts L1 instruction TLB demand accesses whether the access hits or misses in the TLB.

L1I_TLB_PRFM.title: Level 1 instruction TLB software preload
L1I_TLB_PRFM.description: Counts L1 instruction TLB accesses generated by software preload or prefetch instructions. This event counts whether the access hits or misses in the TLB. This event does not count TLB maintenance operations.

DTLB_HWUPD.title: Data TLB hardware update of translation table
DTLB_HWUPD.description: Counts cycles during which there is a data translation table access that caused an update of the translation table.

ITLB_HWUPD.title: Instruction TLB hardware update of translation table
ITLB_HWUPD.description: Counts cycles during which there was an instruction translation table access that caused an update of the translation table.

DTLB_STEP.title: Data TLB translation table walk, step
DTLB_STEP.description: Counts number of page table walks taken for a data translation table miss that caused a refill in the last level TLB.

ITLB_STEP.title: Instruction TLB translation table walk, step
ITLB_STEP.description: Counts number of page table walks taken for an instruction translation table miss that caused a refill in the last level TLB.

DTLB_WALK_LARGE.title: Data TLB large page translation table walk
DTLB_WALK_LARGE.description: Counts data memory accesses caused page translation table walks that yield a large page. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

ITLB_WALK_LARGE.title: Instruction TLB large page translation table walk
ITLB_WALK_LARGE.description: Counts instruction memory access caused page translation table walks that yields a large page. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

DTLB_WALK_SMALL.title: Data TLB small page translation table walk
DTLB_WALK_SMALL.description: Counts data memory access caused page translation table walks that yield a small page. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

ITLB_WALK_SMALL.title: Instruction TLB small page translation table walk
ITLB_WALK_SMALL.description: Counts instruction memory access caused translation table walks that yield a small page. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

DTLB_WALK_RW.title: Data TLB demand access with at least one translation table walk
DTLB_WALK_RW.description: Counts demand data TLB accesses that resulted in at least one translation table walk. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

ITLB_WALK_RD.title: Instruction TLB demand access with at least one translation table walk
ITLB_WALK_RD.description: Counts demand instruction TLB accesses that resulted in at least one translation table walk. Partial translations that also cause a table walk are counted. This event does not count table walks caused by TLB maintenance operations.

L1D_CACHE_RW.title: Level 1 data cache demand access
L1D_CACHE_RW.description: Counts L1 data demand cache accesses from any load or store operation. Near atomic operations that resolve in the CPUs caches counts as both a write access and read access.

L1I_CACHE_RD.title: Level 1 instruction cache demand fetch
L1I_CACHE_RD.description: Counts demand instruction fetches which access the L1 instruction cache.

L1D_CACHE_PRFM.title: Level 1 data cache software preload
L1D_CACHE_PRFM.description: Counts L1 data cache accesses from software preload or prefetch instructions.

L1I_CACHE_PRFM.title: Level 1 instruction cache software preload
L1I_CACHE_PRFM.description: Counts instruction fetches generated by software preload or prefetch instructions which access the L1 instruction cache.

L1I_CACHE_HWPRF.title: Level 1 instruction cache hardware prefetch
L1I_CACHE_HWPRF.description: Counts instruction fetches which access the L1 instruction cache generated by the hardware prefetcher.

L1D_CACHE_REFILL_PRFM.title: Level 1 data cache refill, software preload
L1D_CACHE_REFILL_PRFM.description: Counts L1 data cache refills where the cache line access was generated by software preload or prefetch instructions.

L2D_CACHE_RW.title: Level 2 data cache demand access
L2D_CACHE_RW.description: Counts L2 cache demand accesses from any load/store operations or instruction fetches. L2 cache is a unified cache for data and instruction accesses, and so therefore accesses are either data accesses missed in L1 data cache or instruction fetches missed in the L1 instruction cache.

L2I_CACHE_RD.title: Level 2 instruction cache demand fetch
L2I_CACHE_RD.description: Counts L2 cache accesses that are due to a demand instruction cache access.

L2D_CACHE_PRFM.title: Level 2 data cache software preload
L2D_CACHE_PRFM.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by software preload or prefetch instructions targeting the L2 cache.

L2D_CACHE_REFILL_PRFM.title: Level 2 data cache refill, software preload
L2D_CACHE_REFILL_PRFM.description: Counts L2 cache refills for accesses generated by software preload or prefetch instructions targeting the L2 cache.

L3D_CACHE_PRFM.title: Level 3 data cache software preload
L3D_CACHE_PRFM.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses generated by software preload or prefetch instructions targeting the level 3 cache.

L1D_CACHE_HWPRF.title: Level 1 data cache hardware prefetch
L1D_CACHE_HWPRF.description: Counts L1 data cache accesses from any load/store operations generated by the hardware prefetcher.

L2D_CACHE_HWPRF.title: Level 2 data cache hardware prefetch
L2D_CACHE_HWPRF.description: Counts L2 cache accesses generated by hardware prefetchers targeting the L2 cache.

L3D_CACHE_HWPRF.title: Level 3 data cache hardware prefetch
L3D_CACHE_HWPRF.description: Counts level 3 cache accesses generated by hardware prefetchers targeting the level 3 cache.

LL_CACHE_HWPRF.title: Last level cache hardware prefetch
LL_CACHE_HWPRF.description:
The counter counts each access counted by LL_CACHE that is due to a hardware prefetch.

The hardware prefetch is generated by a hardware prefetcher at the Last level cache.

STALL_FRONTEND_MEMBOUND.title: Frontend stall cycles, memory bound
STALL_FRONTEND_MEMBOUND.description: Counts cycles when the frontend could not send any micro-operations to the dispatch logic stage due to resource constraints in the memory resources.

STALL_FRONTEND_L1I.title: Frontend stall cycles, level 1 instruction cache
STALL_FRONTEND_L1I.description: Counts cycles when the frontend is stalled because there is an instruction fetch request pending in the L1 instruction cache.

STALL_FRONTEND_MEM.title: Frontend stall cycles, last level PE cache or memory
STALL_FRONTEND_MEM.description: Counts cycles when the frontend is stalled because there is an instruction fetch request pending in the last level core cache.

STALL_FRONTEND_TLB.title: Frontend stall cycles, TLB
